target_population: Eligible non-test registered users with a complete seven-day observation window.

assumptions: Each observed row is one eligible user.
The metric is binary after a fixed seven-day observation window.
The Bernoulli probability is interpreted only for the analyzed slice, not as a causal effect.
failure_modes: Segment-level non-response inherited from the sampling audit.

metrics.support: x > 0 after conditioning on positive revenue
metrics.zero_handling: Keep zero-order users as separate mass; fit the lognormal model only to positive amounts.
assumptions: Positive order amounts are right-skewed and non-negative by construction.
Zero amounts are not silently passed into the lognormal fit.
The fitted distribution is a working model for diagnostics, not a guarantee of business truth.
failure_modes: Zeros accidentally included in a lognormal fit.
Too few positive values for a stable shape estimate.

assumptions: The metric is a count in a fixed seven-day user window.
The Poisson mean is an average rate, not a product-quality explanation.
Overdispersion or excess zeros must be checked before using Poisson-based intervals.
Right tail behavior matters for interpretation of the mean.
A lognormal fit is a diagnostic model, not a promise that users follow one homogeneous process.
failure_modes: Mean treated as typical user experience under a visible right tail.
Small samples hiding heavy-tail or mixture behavior.
